      <title>NBA Executives Increasingly Expect Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade As Bucks Signal Desire To Move On</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285651/NBA-Executives-Increasingly-Expect-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Trade-As-Bucks-Signal-Desire-To-Move-On</link>
      <description><p>After years of speculation, the belief around the NBA has shifted with the expectation that the Milwaukee Bucks will trade Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason. League insiders at the draft combine in Chicago described the Bucks as a franchise ready to move on after several disappointing playoff runs and the limbo of Antetokounmpo's status throughout the 2025-26 season.</p>
<p>"It just feels like they're done with the circus, more than anything," an Eastern Conference executive said. "They seem to want a clean break and to move on."</p>
<p>Co-owner Jimmy Haslam set the June 23-24 NBA draft as a target deadline for resolution. Milwaukee's front office must choose between rebuilding through a trade or retaining Antetokounmpo and improving around him. The belief around the league is that if Antetokounmpo remains on the roster when he becomes eligible for a four-year, $275 million extension on October 1, he will sign it.</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo has one season remaining on his contract plus a player option. Recent history shows that star players in similar situations typically influence their own trade destination, affecting their overall value to acquiring teams.</p>
<p>The deals sending Kawhi Leonard from the San Antonio Spurs to the Toronto Raptors in 2018 and Anthony Davis from the New Orleans Pelicans to the Los Angeles Lakers in 2019 serve as the modern blueprint. Both reshuffled the league's balance of power and produced championships for the acquiring franchises.</p>
<p>The NBA's proposed lottery reform adds another variable. Teams in Chicago expect the league to approve a new "3-2-1" system this month, giving up to 18 franchises a realistic shot at the top pick. How that affects Antetokounmpo's trade market remains unclear as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285606/NBAs-New-Lottery-Reform-Expected-To-Make-Teams-More-Reluctant-To-Trade-Future-Firsts">some executives predict teams will be less likely to trade future picks due to the more even odds</a>.</p>
<p>"If someone can get Giannis, they might win the offseason," an East executive said. "For all the distractions and injuries over the last few years, he remains a top-five player in his prime, and those guys aren't available often. That's why this matters so much."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 14:19:57 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285651/NBA-Executives-Increasingly-Expect-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Trade-As-Bucks-Signal-Desire-To-Move-On</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250323.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Executive Says Kawhi Leonard May Be Better Trade Target Than Giannis Antetokounmpo</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285650/Executive-Says-Kawhi-Leonard-May-Be-Better-Trade-Target-Than-Giannis-Antetokounmpo</link>
      <description><p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285574/Bucks-Actively-Fielding-Trade-Offers-For-Giannis-Antetokounmpo">While Giannis Antetokounmpo dominates offseason trade discussion</a>, at least one NBA executive believes the Los Angeles Clippers hold an equally compelling asset in Kawhi Leonard. The Clippers must decide this summer whether to extend, trade or hold Leonard, who has one season at $50 million remaining on his contract.</p>
<p>"Every day you hear about what's going to happen with Giannis, but everyone ignores that Kawhi has been better and healthier over the last two seasons," the Eastern Conference executive said. "If you had a chance to acquire one or the other, I might go Kawhi."</p>
<p>Leonard averaged 28 points on 51% shooting while appearing in 65 games this season, numbers that would typically prompt an immediate extension discussion. His lengthy injury history complicates that calculus and offsets his status as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/player-value-index-rankings/explained">an All-NBA talent this past season</a>.</p>
<p>The Clippers remain under NBA investigation for possible salary cap circumvention, though one Western Conference general manager dismissed the idea of contract nullification.</p>
<p>"There's no way they're voiding his contract even if the league finds them guilty," the GM said. "That would be a reward, clearing the Clippers' books for them."</p>
<p>The consensus at the draft combine favored the Clippers keeping Leonard for 2026-27. Opinion split on whether to extend him or let the season play out.</p>
<p>"I would just play it out and not extend him," a West scout said. "He clearly wants to be in Los Angeles, so I wouldn't be in a rush to lock him in early."</p>
<p>Los Angeles also acquired the fifth overall pick in June's draft via the Indiana Pacers as part of the Ivica Zubac trade, a development one Western Conference executive called significant in shaping the franchise's next move with Leonard.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 14:00:39 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285650/Executive-Says-Kawhi-Leonard-May-Be-Better-Trade-Target-Than-Giannis-Antetokounmpo</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Leonard_Kawhi_lac_250423.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LeBron James Staying With Lakers Considered Most Likely As Market Offers Few Viable Other Options</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285648/LeBron-James-Staying-With-Lakers-Considered-Most-Likely-As-Market-Offers-Few-Viable-Other-Options</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James faces a narrow free agency market this offseason, with league insiders widely expecting him to return to the Los Angeles Lakers as much due to the circumstances as mutual desire between the two parties. Conversations at the NBA draft combine in Chicago pointed to one overriding conclusion that there is no clear alternative fit for James across the league.</p>
<p>"It's just hard to fit him anywhere," a Western Conference scout said.</p>
<p>One Eastern Conference team strategist underscored James' enduring value, telling ESPN his production still ranks among the league's best.</p>
<p>"By our metrics, he remains a top-25 player in the league and if not for his age, we'd probably assess him at near max player level," the strategist said. "Our coaches would tell you he ranks even higher than that when his legs and back are feeling good."</p>
<p>James earned $52 million this season, made the All-Star team and played a pivotal role as the Lakers secured first-round home-court advantage before eliminating the Houston Rockets. James will turn 42 in December, with a presumed 24th season ahead.</p>
<p>Teams with significant cap space, such as the Chicago Bulls and Brooklyn Nets, are unlikely to pursue James. Preferred destinations like the Golden State Warriors lack the financial flexibility to pay him competitively. The Cleveland Cavaliers and New York Knicks are two other teams often linked with James, but they are each currently making deep playoff runs and would have to trade for him to pay a commiserate salary.</p>
<p>That reality leaves the Lakers in a strong negotiating position, but also a delicate one given James' historical stature. One Eastern Conference executive outlined the preferred terms.</p>
<p>"I'd pay LeBron whatever he wants as long as it's a one-year deal, no player option. Give him the no-trade clause," the executive said. "Everything [new Lakers owner Mark] Walter has done so far has been about good business.</p>
<p>"LeBron sells tickets. He keeps the [local] TV partner happy. Re-signing LeBron is good business."</p>
<p>The key question remaining is whether James would accept a midlevel exception contract, projected at just over $15 million for 2026-27, to play elsewhere. Most insiders do not expect that scenario to materialize.</p>
<p>For the Lakers, it is hard to envision many realistic scenarios where they either use their cap space or make a trade that improves their roster in the short-term beyond simply re-signing James, Austin Reaves, Rui Hachimura, Marcus Smart and possibly Luke Kennard.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 12:47:19 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285648/LeBron-James-Staying-With-Lakers-Considered-Most-Likely-As-Market-Offers-Few-Viable-Other-Options</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_260502.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Myles Turner Reveals Lack Of Discipline Under Doc Rivers, Singles Out Giannis For Chronic Tardiness</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285647/Myles-Turner-Reveals-Lack-Of-Discipline-Under-Doc-Rivers-Singles-Out-Giannis-For-Chronic-Tardiness</link>
      <description><p>Milwaukee Bucks center Myles Turner publicly criticized the team's lack of discipline under former coach Doc Rivers, singling out Giannis Antetokounmpo for habitual tardiness during their disappointing 32-50 season. Turner made the remarks on the "Game Recognize Game" podcast with New York Liberty forward Breanna Stewart.</p>
<p>"Guys were late all the time," said Turner. "Guys were showing up to film whenever they wanted to show up. Guys were missing meetings. It was one of the craziest things I personally ever experienced."</p>
<p>Turner identified Antetokounmpo as the primary offender when Stewart asked who was most frequently late.</p>
<p>"Giannis. Giannis is going to show up whenever he wants, really," said Turner. "I think that this kind of just came with the territory that -- and once I saw what was going down, I was like, 'Hey man, more power to you. They ain't going to fine you. S---, do what you do.'"</p>
<p>The pattern extended to team travel. Turner said players routinely arrived an hour late to flights, eventually prompting him to adjust his own schedule accordingly.</p>
<p>Rivers was dismissed April 13 following Milwaukee's first losing season in nine years. ESPN had previously reported a season-long rift between Rivers and the roster, including low-effort practices and a tense team meeting in March.</p>
<p>Turner, who signed a four-year, $108.9 million deal from the Indiana Pacers last offseason, contrasted the experience with his previous stops.</p>
<p>"Any other team I've been on, guys got fined. And there was a sense of order and a sense of understanding," said Turner. "But I personally did not experience that last year for the first time in my career. So we'll see what Taylor Jenkins does, our new coach."</p>
<p>The Bucks hired former Memphis Grizzlies coach Taylor Jenkins to replace Rivers. Meanwhile, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285574/Bucks-Actively-Fielding-Trade-Offers-For-Giannis-Antetokounmpo">Milwaukee is actively seeking trade offers for Antetokounmpo</a>, with co-owner Jimmy Haslam setting the June 23-24 NBA draft as a target deadline for resolution.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 12:30:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285647/Myles-Turner-Reveals-Lack-Of-Discipline-Under-Doc-Rivers-Singles-Out-Giannis-For-Chronic-Tardiness</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Turner_Myles_mil_251103.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Warriors To Open Stephen Curry Extension Talks, Committed To Him Finishing Career With Franchise</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285655/Warriors-To-Open-Stephen-Curry-Extension-Talks-Committed-To-Him-Finishing-Career-With-Franchise</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors plan to open contract extension discussions with Steph Curry this summer, general manager Mike Dunleavy confirmed Friday. Curry, 38, has one year remaining on his current deal worth more than $62 million and becomes extension-eligible in late August.</p>
<p>Curry said last month he would welcome long-term talks. Dunleavy left little ambiguity about the organization's intentions.</p>
<p>"Yeah, that day will come," said Dunleavy. "But I think we've always stated we want Steph to finish his career here, be here for as long as he wants. I'm sure those conversations can happen, and you can predict where they'll be going. Yeah, I don't see under any scenario where we don't want to figure out a way to have him finish here."</p>
<p>Golden State's desire to retain Curry long-term comes as the franchise navigates a transitional offseason. The team finished 37-45, missed the playoffs, and faces pressing decisions on Draymond Green's player option, free agent center Kristaps Porzingis, and injured wings Jimmy Butler and Moses Moody.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/player-value-index-rankings/explained">Curry has remained one of the NBA's best players when healthy</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 23:41:43 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285655/Warriors-To-Open-Stephen-Curry-Extension-Talks-Committed-To-Him-Finishing-Career-With-Franchise</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_260416.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Warriors Expected To Keep 11th Pick To Add Young Talent To Aging Roster</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285653/Warriors-Expected-To-Keep-11th-Pick-To-Add-Young-Talent-To-Aging-Roster</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors front office is leaning toward keeping its 11th overall pick in an upcoming NBA draft widely considered strong through its lottery portion, sources told ESPN. The Warriors, carrying one of the oldest rosters in the league, are focused on adding younger talent to reinforce their depth.</p>
<p>General manager Mike Dunleavy addressed reporters in San Francisco on Friday, stating every option remains open. Trading up, trading back, using the pick outright, or attaching it to a deal for a proven player are all under consideration.</p>
<p>The selection could factor into any pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo of the Milwaukee Bucks, or a potential trade for Kawhi Leonard of the Los Angeles Clippers. Either scenario would likely require the 11th pick as a central asset.</p>
<p>Despite those possibilities, the prevailing organizational preference points toward retention. The front office views the draft as the clearest path to bringing younger legs alongside 38-year-old Steph Curry.</p>
<p>Steve Kerr made clear he is fully prepared to develop whoever Golden State selects.</p>
<p>"Totally," said Kerr, when asked if he'd be committed to playing a rookie. "I've talked to Mike. I don't know the draft, but he feels really strongly we're going to get a good player. It could be a 19-year-old and it could be someone older. ... That guy has to play. He's got to earn it. But we're committed to the development of our young players."</p>
<p>Dunleavy described the draft class as deep through the lottery, reinforcing confidence that the 11th selection carries genuine value regardless of how the organization ultimately proceeds.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 23:32:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285653/Warriors-Expected-To-Keep-11th-Pick-To-Add-Young-Talent-To-Aging-Roster</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/DunleavyJr_Mike_gsw_240112.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Isaiah Thomas Hired By Celtics As Pro, College Scout</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285639/Isaiah-Thomas-Hired-By-Celtics-As-Pro-College-Scout</link>
      <description><p>Isaiah Thomas has returned to the Boston Celtics organization as a pro and college scout, according to league sources.</p>
<p>Thomas, 37, will be based in the Seattle area, his hometown, providing president of basketball operations Brad Stevens with additional coverage on the West Coast. The Celtics' regional scouting staff also includes former Hawks executive Derek Pierce in Atlanta and Keandre Ashley in Dallas.</p>
<p>Thomas was present at the NBA Combine in Chicago this week, where he assisted in prospect interviews and began learning the front-office evaluation process, sources said.</p>
<p>Acquired from the Phoenix Suns in February 2015, Thomas quickly became central to Boston's rebuild. Stevens has credited him with helping build the culture that shaped the Celtics into one of the NBA's most accomplished franchises of the past decade.</p>
<p>Thomas earned All-Star selections in 2015-16 and 2016-17. In the latter season he finished fifth in MVP voting and earned second-team All-NBA honors, winning over fans with an aggressive, relentless style that defied his size.</p>
<p>However, his time in Boston ended on difficult terms as Thomas played through a serious hip injury during the 2017 playoffs before being shut down for the final three games of a conference finals loss to the Cleveland Cavaliers. Thomas was traded to the Cleveland Cavaliers a few months later in the deal that brought Kyrie Irving to Boston.</p>
<p>Hip problems limited Thomas for the remainder of his playing career. He had stints with nine franchises before his final NBA appearance with Phoenix in the 2023-24 season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 22:28:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285639/Isaiah-Thomas-Hired-By-Celtics-As-Pro-College-Scout</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Thomas_Isaiah_bos_150225.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jazz Have Reached Out To Wizards About Trading For No. 1 Pick</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285644/Jazz-Have-Reached-Out-To-Wizards-About-Trading-For-No-1-Pick</link>
      <description><p>The Utah Jazz have reached out to the Washington Wizards over potentially trading for the No. 1 pick in the upcoming NBA Draft, sources told Marc J. Spears of Andscape.</p>
<p>The Jazz would then presumably select BYU's AJ Dybantsa with the top pick.</p>
<p>Wizards president Michael Winger previously said that the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285561/Wizards-Jazz-Open-To-Trading-Down-From-Top-Two-Picks-After-NBA-Draft-Lottery">team would consider trading down</a> to accumulate assets. </p>
<p>Dybantsa averaged 25.5 points and 6.8 rebounds at BYU last season. </p>
<p>The top four picks are expected to be Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer and Caleb Wilson in some order.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 01:45:36 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285644/Jazz-Have-Reached-Out-To-Wizards-About-Trading-For-No-1-Pick</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ainge_Austin_uta_260112.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Steve Kerr 'Thrilled' To Continue Coaching, Vows 'To Be Better' Next Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285652/Steve-Kerr-Thrilled-To-Continue-Coaching-Vows-To-Be-Better-Next-Season</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr will coach the Golden State Warriors for a 13th season after a period of deliberation that included an honest personal evaluation. Kerr, who has won four NBA championships as a head coach, openly admitted his work this past season fell short of his own standards.</p>
<p>"I know I have to be better," said Kerr. "I didn't have a great coaching year."</p>
<p>Kerr and general manager Mike Dunleavy addressed reporters in San Francisco on Friday. The Warriors finished 37-45 and missed the playoffs, a result Dunleavy described as "pretty underwhelming."</p>
<p>Turnover problems were central assessments for both Kerr and Dunleavy. Golden State averaged 15.7 turnovers per game, tied for third highest in the NBA. Kerr said he has since reviewed every turnover from the season.</p>
<p>"We got a little too loose," Kerr said. "Literally loose with the ball. But because of our age, because of our injuries, we spent a lot of time resting. So I've got to tighten the ship up next year."</p>
<p>Kerr spent roughly one week after the season deciding whether to continue coaching. A conversation with his wife, Margot, helped clarify his intentions. A subsequent two-hour meeting with Dunleavy and controlling owner Joe Lacob solidified the decision.</p>
<p>"We had to come to a much more reality-based shared vision on what success looks like for us," said Kerr. "What are we trying to accomplish? Because for the first time since our injury-plagued year (2019-20), we aren't sitting here saying, 'Hey, we can win a championship.' I don't need to say that. We know that right this second. But we had to hash that out. We had to talk about those things."</p>
<p>Kerr emphasized he is neither tired nor burned out, and plans to maintain closer contact with players and staff throughout the summer.</p>
<p>"I'm thrilled to be back," Kerr said. "I couldn't be more excited to continue on this job."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 23:27:22 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285652/Steve-Kerr-Thrilled-To-Continue-Coaching-Vows-To-Be-Better-Next-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_211228.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bryce Cotton To Play For Australia In World Cup Qualifiers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285646/Bryce-Cotton-To-Play-For-Australia-In-World-Cup-Qualifiers</link>
      <description><p>Bryce Cotton has spent years playing professionally in Australia. Now, Cotton will suit up for Australia's national team for FIBA World Cup qualifiers this summer.</p>
<p>Cotten became a naturalized citizen of Australia last fall. That allowed him to earn a spot to suit up for Australia on the national team level.</p>
<p>"Representing the Australian men's national basketball team for the first time is something I don't take lightly," Cotton said.</p>
<p>"Coming from where I come from, this opportunity means a lot to me and my family. Australia has embraced me from day one, and I'm grateful for the chance to wear the green and gold alongside a great group of guys. I'm excited, motivated, and ready to give everything I have for the country."</p>
<p>In major events, each country is allowed to roster only one naturalized player. Australia will have to choose between Cotton and veteran NBA wing Matisse Thybulle for the World Cup and future Olympics. Both Cotton and Thybulle are Americans who were naturalized by Australia.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 08:22:42 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285646/Bryce-Cotton-To-Play-For-Australia-In-World-Cup-Qualifiers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_RealGM_Basketball.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Terry Stotts, Jerry Stackhouse Leaving Warriors; Willie Green Among Candidates For Assistant Job</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285643/Terry-Stotts-Jerry-Stackhouse-Leaving-Warriors;-Willie-Green-Among-Candidates-For-Assistant-Job</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors are losing lassistant Terry Stotts and defensive coordinator Jerry Stackhouse this offseason. The moves come despite the agreement to retain Steve Kerr as head coach on a two-year contract.</p>
<p>Both assistants completed two seasons under Kerr before their contracts expired. Stotts notified Kerr late in the regular season that he would not return.</p>
<p>"I enjoyed my time with Steve, the staff and players," said Stotts. "My two years there were fulfilling. Nothing but well-wishes."</p>
<p>Stotts brings nearly three decades of coaching experience, including nine seasons leading the Portland Trail Blazers. He remains open to NBA head coaching positions elsewhere.</p>
<p>Stackhouse joined the Warriors in July 2024, replacing Kenny Atkinson following Atkinson's move to the Cleveland Cavaliers. Prior to Golden State, Stackhouse spent five seasons as head coach at Vanderbilt. He is also departing to pursue head coaching opportunities.</p>
<p>The staff changes extend beyond Stotts and Stackhouse. Assistant Chris DeMarco left in January to become head coach of the New York Liberty, with veteran assistant Kris Weems stepping up and expected to remain on staff.</p>
<p>Former New Orleans Pelicans head coach Willie Green has emerged as a candidate to fill one of the vacancies, per league sources. Green began his NBA coaching career with Golden State in 2016, serving three seasons under Kerr. Green and Kerr remain close.</p>
<p>The Warriors finished last season with 37 wins, missing the playoffs entirely. Kerr recently signed a two-year contract extension, remaining the NBA's highest-paid coach. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 23:22:36 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285643/Terry-Stotts-Jerry-Stackhouse-Leaving-Warriors;-Willie-Green-Among-Candidates-For-Assistant-Job</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Stotts_Terry_por_130106.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Williams Ready For West Finals After Missing Six Games With Hamstring Strain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285656/Jalen-Williams-Ready-For-West-Finals-After-Missing-Six-Games-With-Hamstring-Strain</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Williams declared himself fully healthy for the Western Conference finals Friday, ending uncertainty over the Oklahoma City Thunder wing's availability after a six-game absence. Williams sustained a Grade 1 left hamstring strain during the second round against the Phoenix Suns.</p>
<p>Williams addressed his status in a video posted to his YouTube channel, explaining that Oklahoma City's 3-0 series lead over the Los Angeles Lakers gave him additional recovery time beyond his original timeline.</p>
<p>"It's good that I haven't had to rush back from my hamstring stuff at all," said Williams. "I'm actually taking like extra days now than from what was originally planned because we were up 3-0 [over the Lakers], so there was no point in going into this series and possibly hurting myself before we play the Timberwolves or the Spurs.</p>
<p>"I'm about to go into another series healthy."</p>
<p>Mark Daigneault, speaking after Friday's practice, said Williams was "progressing" but offered no further detail. Williams was dressed out for the session. Oklahoma City is not required to update his official status until the evening before the series opener.</p>
<p>Injuries have been a persistent factor for Williams this season. He missed the first 19 games recovering from right wrist surgery and later dealt with two extended absences due to a right hamstring strain. He appeared in just 33 regular season games.</p>
<p>Ajay Mitchell filled Williams' starting role effectively during the Lakers series, averaging 22.5 points and 6.0 assists across four games in the sweep.</p>
<p>Williams, a third-team All-NBA selection during the 2024-25 season, posted 22 points, seven rebounds and six assists in the playoff opener against Phoenix before exiting Game 2 with the hamstring injury after 23 minutes.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 15 May 2026 23:48:55 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285656/Jalen-Williams-Ready-For-West-Finals-After-Missing-Six-Games-With-Hamstring-Strain</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Williams_Jalen_okc_260501.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Warriors Expect Draymond Green To Return But Defer To His Decision</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285654/Warriors-Expect-Draymond-Green-To-Return-But-Defer-To-His-Decision</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors want Draymond Green back, but the decision rests entirely with the veteran forward. General manager Mike Dunleavy made clear Friday that the organization has expressed its preference while leaving the choice to Green.</p>
<p>"I think we have had discussions where we want him to finish his career (as) a Warrior," said Dunleavy. "He kind of feels the same way. I would expect him to be back, but it's his call on that."</p>
<p>Green holds a player option worth just over $27 million for next season. The Warriors could alternatively ask him to decline it in favor of a longer-term deal at a reduced annual salary. His contract could also serve as salary-matching currency if Golden State pursues a star in a trade this summer.</p>
<p>Steve Kerr echoed Dunleavy's confidence in a continued partnership.</p>
<p>"The number one thing is, it's up to him," said Kerr. "He has the player option, so he has to go through that with his agent, how that plays out. I am committed to coaching him, coaching Steph (Curry), obviously, for as long as they're here. I look at them as collaborators, and what we've built is pretty special."</p>
<p>Green must exercise or decline his option before June 29. His choice will carry significant weight in shaping Golden State's roster and trade flexibility heading into the offseason.</p></description>

      <title>76ers Begin GM Search With Bob Myers Emphasizing 'Harmony' In Next Hire</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285642/76ers-Begin-GM-Search-With-Bob-Myers-Emphasizing-Harmony-In-Next-Hire</link>
      <description><p>The Philadelphia 76ers are seeking a general manager who will work in concert with others throughout the organization. Bob Myers, the former Golden State Warriors GM overseeing the search, made that priority clear Thursday.</p>
<p>"You need good people that are in harmony," Myers said at a news conference.</p>
<p>The 76ers dismissed Daryl Morey on Tuesday after six seasons in which the team posted a 270-212 regular season record and reached the playoffs five times but never advanced past the second round. Philadelphia was swept by the New York Knicks in this year's second round.</p>
<p>Myers said he hopes to complete the hire before the NBA Draft, which begins June 23, but will not rush the process.</p>
<p>"I'm a big believer in character and leadership," he said. "I'm looking for a person who embodies those things."</p>
<p>The incoming GM will inherit a roster that has pivoted to be built around Tyrese Maxey and VJ Edgecombe as the backcourt of the present and future, as opposed to Joel Embiid and Paul George, two high-priced veterans whose availability has been inconsistent. Embiid has dealt with recurring injuries. George served a 25-game suspension this season for violating the league's drug policy. Myers said roster depth will be a central question during the search. "Can this model work?" he said.</p>
<p>Harris confirmed the organization will support spending beyond the luxury tax threshold and reaffirmed coach Nick Nurse's standing.</p>
<p>"He's earned the right to be here," Harris said. "He's our coach moving forward."</p>
<p>Myers, who won four championships with Golden State, cautioned that building a title contender demands sustained effort.</p>
<p>"It takes a great level of uncomfortability to win a championship," he said. "There is no wave of a wand. This requires a ton of work."</p></description>

      <title>NBA Last Two Minute Report Backs No-Call On Jarrett Allen At End Of Game 5</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285641/NBA-Last-Two-Minute-Report-Backs-No-Call-On-Jarrett-Allen-At-End-Of-Game-5</link>
      <description><p>The NBA ruled Thursday that officials made the correct decision by not whistling Jarrett Allen for a foul in the closing seconds of regulation in Game 5 between the Cleveland Cavaliers and Detroit Pistons. The no-call drew immediate criticism from Detroit's bench and players after Cleveland's 117-113 overtime win Wednesday night.</p>
<p>The disputed play occurred with the score tied 103-103. Allen made contact with Pistons guard Ausar Thompson as both players pursued a loose ball. Had a foul been called, Thompson would have gone to the free throw line in a tied game. Instead, Cleveland secured the ball and went on to win in overtime, taking a 3-2 series lead.</p>
<p>The league addressed the sequence in its Last Two Minute Report.</p>
<p>"Allen and Thompson legally step to the same spot while pursuing the loose ball before either player has possession, and both lose their balance from the marginal contact," the report stated.</p>
<p>Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff disputed the ruling after the loss. "Allen fouled Ausar -- clearly. He tripped him when he was going for a loose ball," Bickerstaff said.</p>
<p>Crew chief Tony Brothers explained the decision on the floor. "During live play, both players were going for the ball, and there was incidental contact with the legs with no player having possession of the ball," Brothers said via pool report.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 22:46:28 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Allen_Jarrett_cle_221115.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Knicks Finals Run Could Produce Up To $180M In Gross Revenue</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285640/Knicks-Finals-Run-Could-Produce-Up-To-$180M-In-Gross-Revenue</link>
      <description><p>The New York Knicks' second straight trip to the Eastern Conference finals is producing a major financial windfall for Madison Square Garden Sports, the publicly traded company that owns the Knicks and the NHL's Rangers. Gross playoff revenue could reach $180 million if the team advances deep into the NBA Finals.</p>
<p>The Knicks have already generated close to $50 million in gross ticket revenue from five home playoff games, before the NBA takes its share. Seaport Research Partners analyst David Joyce, who covers MSG Sports, estimates each home game in the second round was worth $12 million across tickets, suites, concessions, and merchandise, up from $8 million per game in the first round.</p>
<p>Joyce projects conference finals home games are worth roughly $17 million each. NBA Finals games would likely exceed $20 million per contest. A full seven-game series against the Cleveland Cavaliers combined with a maximum of three home Finals games would push total gross revenue near $180 million. A shorter path would still produce an estimated $140 million.</p>
<p>For comparison, the Knicks generated $115 million in total playoff revenue in 2025, according to Joyce. The NHL's Edmonton Oilers set a league record with $15 million per home game in ticket revenue during last year's Stanley Cup Finals rematch against the Florida Panthers.</p>
<p>Joyce estimates the Knicks' profit margin on playoff revenue is approximately 55% after the NBA's cut and other costs. The league reduced its share of playoff ticket revenue from 45% to 25% ahead of the 2011-12 season, meaningfully improving the financial opportunity for deep postseason runs.</p>
<p>The Knicks are the only remaining playoff team ranked in the NBA's top 10 for gate revenue. That distinction matters beyond one franchise. Playoff revenue feeds into basketball-related income, the figure used to determine the league-wide revenue split between owners and players under the collective bargaining agreement.</p>
<p>MSG Sports shares have risen roughly 5% over the past month, trailing an 8% gain for the S&P 500 over the same period, though the stock closed Wednesday at an all-time high. The Knicks carry a $9.85 billion valuation in Sportico's most recent NBA rankings. Combined with the Rangers' $3.65 billion NHL valuation, the two franchises represent a $13.5 billion asset base, roughly 48% above MSGS's current enterprise value.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 22:40:20 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Towns_Karlanthony_nyk_260420.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>NBA Soft-Tissue Injuries Have Surged As Players Try To Keep Up With Demands Of Modern Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285638/NBA-Soft-Tissue-Injuries-Have-Surged-As-Players-Try-To-Keep-Up-With-Demands-Of-Modern-Game</link>
      <description><p>Soft-tissue leg injuries have been one of the key developments of the 2026 NBA playoffs, as they were last year, and the numbers behind the trend are striking. Documented calf injuries across the entire league stood at 18 in 2010-11. Last season, that figure reached 60 and 86 this season. </p>
<p>Jalen Williams has missed six Oklahoma City Thunder playoff games with a hamstring strain. Franz Wagner, OG Anunoby, Aaron Gordon, Peyton Watson, and Jayson Tatum have all missed time with calf or hamstring issues. Donte DiVincenzo ruptured his Achilles. Luka Doncic missed the Los Angeles Lakers' entire playoff run with a Grade 2 hamstring tear. Anthony Edwards has been limited since returning from a knee injury.</p>
<p>Researchers and sports scientists point to a fundamental shift in how basketball is played. Ron Adams, a longtime NBA assistant coach, framed the issue directly.</p>
<p>"Basketball used to be a two-footed sport," said Adams. "If you ever watched a John Wooden practice, it was always the same: get to the paint and play off of two feet. Nowadays, the game is a one-footed sport. Most players are making every move off of one foot."</p>
<p>The stepback three and the Euro-step, now central to modern NBA offense, place extreme demand on the calf and hamstring. Richard Lieber, chief scientific officer at the Shirley Ryan AbilityLab and author of the standard textbook on skeletal muscle, describes the core mechanism: muscle damage occurs when a muscle is both activated and stretched simultaneously. The stepback does exactly that, forcing rapid eccentric loading on a single leg.</p>
<p>The star players most associated with one-footed creation have paid a physical cost. Jayson Tatum attempted 11 stepback threes in his 2017-18 rookie season. In 2024-25, he attempted 251, a 23-fold rise. Only five players have surpassed 1,000 career stepback threes since tracking began in 2013-14: James Harden, Doncic, Stephen Curry, Tatum, and Damian Lillard. Lillard and Tatum have each suffered Achilles tears. Doncic has sustained three calf strains. Curry recorded the first muscle strain of his 17-year career in last year's playoffs.</p>
<p>The recurring-injury numbers are particularly concerning. This season, 13 players sustained multiple calf injuries. Six players suffered three or more calf strains in a single season: Coby White, Rui Hachimura, Isaiah Hartenstein, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Evan Mobley, and Ty Jerome. From 2010 through 2024, that had happened a total of five times across 14 seasons combined. Calf injuries now account for nearly one in four lower-leg injuries league-wide, up from fewer than one in ten a decade ago. Last season, six players tore their Achilles, the highest single-season total on record.</p>
<p>The league's schedule compounds the strain. An 82-game regular season feeds into a two-month playoff run, with no meaningful structural change to reduce physical wear. The NBA introduced an in-season tournament in 2023 and added a 65-game eligibility threshold for award consideration, moves that increase competitive pressure rather than reduce it. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 17:54:09 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285638/NBA-Soft-Tissue-Injuries-Have-Surged-As-Players-Try-To-Keep-Up-With-Demands-Of-Modern-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Tatum_Jayson_250503.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Brunson Deflects 'Star' Question When Discussing Acceptance Of Off-Ball Role</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285637/Jalen-Brunson-Deflects-Star-Question-When-Discussing-Acceptance-Of-Off-Ball-Role</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Brunson immediately accepted a reduced ball-handling role when coach Mike Brown proposed running more offense through Karl-Anthony Towns during the playoffs, a shift that has since produced seven consecutive New York Knicks victories.</p>
<p>The adjustment came after New York dropped two straight games to the Atlanta Hawks in their first round series, putting the team in danger of an early postseason exit. Brown approached players about redistributing offensive responsibilities, with Brunson moving off the ball more frequently.</p>
<p>"Was there any concern? No. What was the dialogue? The dialogue was, 'OK, let's do it,'" Brunson said Wednesday. "It was that simple. There's not really much you can talk about at that point when you're down 2-1."</p>
<p>When a reporter suggested other star players might have resisted, Brunson replied: "1) I'm not a star. 2) I want to win." He added while leaving: "I'm not self-centered, that's why."</p>
<p>New York has outscored opponents by an average of 26.4 points during the streak, posting a 130.5 offensive rating per 100 possessions. Towns has recorded 66 postseason assists, 44 more than his total from a year ago.</p>
<p>Brunson's own production has climbed as well. He is averaging 27.3 points on 51.9 percent shooting and 42.6 percent from three during the streak. His scoring efficiency per touch rose from .285 during the regular season to .324.</p>
<p>"Putting KAT in that position knowing we have great screeners and great cutters, and like I said a great facilitator, it was easier to say, 'Let's try it and do it more,'" Brown said.</p>
<p>Brunson's buy-in mirrors his 2024 contract extension, a four-year, $156.5 million deal in which he accepted less than market value, helping New York avoid the second apron and strengthen its roster depth.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 15:18:01 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brunson_Jalen_nyk_260501.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Steve Kerr Decided To Retire As Warriors Coach Before Changing His Mind After Play-In Win</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285634/Steve-Kerr-Decided-To-Retire-As-Warriors-Coach-Before-Changing-His-Mind-After-Play-In-Win</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr had made up his mind he was going to retire as Golden State's head coach after 12 seasons regardless of the outcome of their play-in game against the Los Angeles Clippers in April. Kerr put the odds at 95%, but then the Warriors won, and everything changed.</p>
<p>"I'm not leaving," Kerr told his staff in the locker room after the Clippers victory.  Kerr's wife, Margot Kerr, sent him a text that said the same thing.</p>
<p>The reversal came after months of genuine internal conflict. As far back as June 2025, Kerr had begun discussing retirement with his wife. He sensed the dynasty had run its course and worried about overstaying his welcome, having watched mentor Gregg Popovich struggle to walk away from the San Antonio Spurs before a stroke ultimately made the decision for him.</p>
<p>"I realized he couldn't do it," Kerr said of Popovich. "He couldn't walk away."</p>
<p>By the time the Warriors reached the play-in round, Kerr had grown increasingly certain his time was done. He told staff privately his decision was nearly final. But the victory over the Clippers, a furious comeback fueled by Al Horford hitting four straight three-pointers and Stephen Curry making the decisive play, reminded him of everything he stood to leave behind.</p>
<p>The following week he drove to Curry's home in Atherton to get his star player's read on the situation. He met with owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy. His daughter Maddy scolded him for dwelling on what he didn't like about the job rather than the hundred things he did. Margot wanted him to stay, partly because it kept their son Nick and his family close by.</p>
<p>The competing emotional pulls were real and he acknowledged them directly.</p>
<p>"Coaching has unlocked the best version of myself," Kerr said. "I think I'm scared that I will lose that daily engagement and purpose that not only feeds my soul, but helps me deal with my literal chronic daily pain."</p>
<p>Lacob told Kerr he wanted him back. The two men, who had disagreed on some things during the season, shared a conviction that what the Warriors had built was worth protecting and fighting for. Kerr told Dunleavy he was in.</p>
<p>Kerr called Curry with the news and the two spoke about the road ahead. Draymond Green went on the "Inside the NBA" set and echoed the themes Kerr had outlined in a personal letter he had written to Green during a difficult stretch mid-season, a letter centered on loyalty, fighting spirit and the obligation to set the franchise up for success when the current core was gone.</p>
<p>Kerr laughed when he reflected on the full arc of his deliberations.</p>
<p>"I'm just like Riley," he said, referencing Pat Riley's famous inability to step away from the game.</p>
<p>Kerr had fielded numerous offers through his agent, including opportunities to become a team president or a return to broadcasting. </p>
<p>"I'm not a suit," Kerr said of running a team's front office as he did previously with the Phoenix Suns. "I want the whistle."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 12:53:35 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285634/Steve-Kerr-Decided-To-Retire-As-Warriors-Coach-Before-Changing-His-Mind-After-Play-In-Win</guid>

   </item>

      <description><p>Jalen Duren has struggled during the Detroit Pistons' playoff run and the situation came to a head in the fourth quarter of Game 5 when he was benched by J.B. Bickerstaff in favor of Paul Reed Jr.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/stats/2026/Advanced_Stats/Qualified/points/All/desc/1/Playoffs">Duren has a PER of 13.4 during the playoffs</a> compared to 25.8 during the regular season in what will likely be an All-NBA award ahead of his restricted free agency.</p>
<p>Duren took accountability for his play when speaking to the media postgame.</p>
<p>"I feel I can be better in all aspects of the game," said Duren. "Continue to grind and continue to grow."</p>
<p>Duren was supportive of Reed and ultimately just wants the Pistons to win.</p>
<p>"As long as we get the win, I'm good," said Duren. "Obviously it didn't come out the way we want it today."</p></description>

<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285477/Bulls-Pursuing-Celtics-Assistant-GM-Dave-Lewin-For-Front-Office-Role">The Bulls were also interested in hiring Dave Lewin for the job</a> after they were impressed with him during the interview process for the top position that went to Graham.</p>
<p>Chicago will also hire Acie Law as Vice President of Player Personnel. Law and Graham played collegiately together at Texas A&M.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285630/Makar-Gevorkian-Promoted-By-Nets-To-Assistant-GM</link>
      <description><p>Makar Gevorkian has been promoted by the Brooklyn Nets to assistant general manager. Gevorkian was elevated to the role following two seasons as vice president of basketball operations alignment & strategic planning.</p>
<p>"Makar's strategic, forward-thinking mind, along with his comprehensive knowledge of the league's salary cap structure, have made him a valued voice within our front office and we are excited to elevate him to this well-earned role," said Nets General Manager Sean Marks.</p>
<p>Recently completing his sixth season with the Nets, Gevorkian joined the franchise in 2020 as a basketball operations assistant. He went on to serve one season as a salary cap and strategy associate and two seasons as the director of salary cap before being promoted to his most recent position in 2024. Gevorkian earned his law degree from the University of Chicago and was admitted to the California Bar after graduating from Loyola Marymount University with a bachelor's degree in economics and a minor in applied mathematics.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Ebuka Okorie, Henri Veesaar, Isaiah Evans, Jack Kayil Staying In Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285627/Ebuka-Okorie-Henri-Veesaar-Isaiah-Evans-Jack-Kayil-Staying-In-Draft</link>
      <description><p>As the 2026 NBA Draft Combine progresses this week, several Early Entry Candidates are making decisions on their future. Ebuka Okorie of Stanford, Henri Veesaar of North Carolina, Isaiah Evans of Duke and Germany's Jack Kayil have all shared that they are staying in the 2026 NBA Draft.</p>
<p>Okorie averaged 23.2 points, 3.6 rebounds, 3.6 assists and 1.6 steals as a freshman for the Cardinal. The 6-foot-2 points guard shot 46.5% from the field, 35.4% on three-pointers and 83.2% on free throws.</p>
<p>Veesaar had a breakout season for the Tar Heels as a junior. The 7-foot big man out of Estonia averaged 17 points, 8.7 rebounds, 2.1 assists and 1.2 blocks per game. Veesaar also shot 61% from the field, including 42.6% on 3.0 three-point attempts per game.</p>
<p>Evans doubled his production across the board in his sophomore season with the Blue Devils. The 6-foot-6 wing averaged 15 points and 3.2 rebounds per game, while shooting 36% on 7.4 three-point attempts per game.</p>
<p>Kayil spent last season with Alba Berlin in Germany, after playing the previous year in Serbia. The 6-foot-5 guard averaged 12 points, 2.9 rebounds and 3.7 assists in 50 games across all competitions.</p></description>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Allen Graves To Stay In Draft If Considered First Rounder; Will Consider LSU, Duke If Returning To NCAA</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285626/Allen-Graves-To-Stay-In-Draft-If-Considered-First-Rounder;-Will-Consider-LSU-Duke-If-Returning-To-NCAA</link>
      <description><p>Santa Clara forward Allen Graves is focused on excelling at the NBA Draft Combine this week. If Graves is considered a likely first-round pick, he's going to stay in the 2026 NBA Draft. If the freshman forward chooses to return to college, he's considering LSU and Duke. Graves mentioned that he's also heard from Kentucky, but that the Tigers and Blue Devils are his top choices.</p>
<p>The 6-foot-9 forward excelled as a redshirt freshman for the Broncos last season. In 35 games. Graves averaged 11.8 points, 6.5 rebounds, 1.9 steals and nearly a block per game. Graves shot 51% from the field, 41% from behind the arc and 75% at the free throw line.</p></description>
